title: Umami Flaky Japanese Lentil Stew
description: A hearty, umami‑rich Japanese‑style stew featuring flaky French green lentils, aromatic vegetables, and a savory miso‑soy glaze. This one‑pot dish is both kosher‑friendly and bursting with comforting flavors.
instructions: Rinse the lentils under cold water and set aside.
Heat sesame oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the diced onion, minced garlic, and minced ginger, sautéing until the onion becomes translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add sliced shiitake mushrooms, diced carrot, and zucchini to the pot and cook for another 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Deglaze the pot by pouring in the sake and mirin, stirring to lift any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Stir in the rinsed lentils, vegetable broth, soy sauce, and miso paste. Bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to a simmer, cover, and let cook for 20–25 minutes, or until the lentils are tender and the stew has thickened slightly.
Stir in toasted sesame seeds and taste, adjusting seasoning with additional soy sauce if needed.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped cilantro. Optionally sprinkle a few nori flakes for extra umami.
Serve immediately with steamed rice or crusty bread for a complete meal.
